title: Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled

seo.title: Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled: Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled at home! Our recipe guides you through making juicy, flavorful al pastor pork on a vertical spit. Perfect for any meal.

seo.imageAlt: Delicious Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled with pineapple and cilantro
seo.imageCaption: Experience the authentic taste of Mexico with our homemade Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled, piled high with fresh toppings.

description: Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled are a culinary masterpiece from Mexico, featuring thinly sliced pork marinated in a vibrant adobo sauce and cooked to perfection on a vertical spit. The result is incredibly tender, flavorful meat with crispy edges, traditionally served in warm tortillas with pineapple, onion, and cilantro. This method captures the authentic street food experience right in your own home.

ingredients.notes: sliced into rounds, plus small pieces for spit

instructions.title: Prepare the Chile Marinade
instructions.text: Rehydrate the guajillo and ancho chiles by simmering them in hot water for 10-15 minutes until softened. Drain the chiles. In a blender, combine the rehydrated chiles, achiote paste, chipotle pepper and adobo sauce, white vinegar, pineapple juice, garlic, chopped onion, cumin, Mexican oregano, and salt. Blend until a smooth, thick marinade forms. Add a little water if needed to achieve desired consistency.

instructions.title: Marinate the Pork
instructions.text: Place the thinly sliced pork shoulder in a large bowl or a resealable bag. Pour the prepared adobo marinade over the pork, ensuring every piece is thoroughly coated. Massage the marinade into the meat. Cover the bowl or seal the bag and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to fully develop.

instructions.title: Assemble the Trompo (Spit)
instructions.text: If using a vertical spit (trompo), place a thick slice of pineapple at the base. Begin carefully stacking the marinated pork slices onto the spit, layering them tightly to form a cylindrical shape. Try to keep the stack even. Finish by placing another slice of pineapple on top of the pork. This will help keep the meat moist and add flavor.

instructions.title: Spit Grill the Pork
instructions.text: Preheat your grill or oven with the vertical spit attachment to medium-high heat (around 350-375F or 175-190C). Place the assembled trompo onto the spit rotisserie. Cook, rotating constantly, for 30-45 minutes, or until the pork is deeply caramelized on the outside and cooked through. The exact time will depend on the thickness of your pork stack and heat source. Use a meat thermometer to ensure internal temperature reaches 145F (63C).

instructions.title: Slice and Rest the Meat
instructions.text: Once the pork is cooked, carefully remove the trompo from the heat. Using a sharp knife, shave thin slices of the outer, crispy pork directly from the spit, allowing any juices to drip down onto the remaining meat. Continue to rotate and shave as needed. Let the sliced pork rest for a few minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare and Serve Tacos
instructions.text: While the pork rests, warm the corn tortillas on a comal or dry skillet until pliable and lightly toasted. To assemble the tacos, place a generous portion of the shaved al pastor pork onto warm tortillas. Garnish with finely diced white onion, fresh chopped cilantro, and small pieces of grilled pineapple. Serve immediately with lime wedges and your favorite salsa.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, allow the pork to marinate for at least 12-24 hours. The longer it marinates, the deeper the flavor will be.
If you don't have a vertical spit, you can roast the marinated pork in an oven on a baking sheet, turning occasionally, or grill it in batches on skewers.
Always warm your tortillas just before serving. This makes them pliable, enhances their flavor, and prevents them from breaking when folded.
article: Tacos al Pastor Spit Grilled is a beloved Mexican Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
